block-definition.comment: Put this at the TOP of your drawing script. Snap! paints pen trails as pixels unless it is told to keep them as vectors as well - this switches that on and empties the trail, so what you draw next can be sent to Sangala Studio as lines a machine can follow. (It is the same setting as "Log pen vectors" in Snap!'s settings menu.)

block.l: StageMorph.prototype.enablePenLogging = true; var stage = world.children[0].stage; stage.trailsLog = []; return 'logging';

block-definition.comment: Sends what the turtle drew to Sangala Studio, which puts it on the mat. THREE THINGS MUST BE TRUE: Sangala Studio is running; Snap! was opened from http://extensions.snap.berkeley.edu (plain http - a secure page is not allowed to talk to a program on your own machine); and "JavaScript extensions" is ticked in Snap!'s settings menu. THE PEN COLOUR IS THE INSTRUCTION: a BLUE stroke becomes a fold line (perforated); a stroke of any other colour becomes a cut line - or a drawn line, if there is a pen in the tool holder instead of a blade. Nothing moves on its own: a human still presses Make It in Sangala Studio.

block.l: // Start the send. It must be ASYNCHRONOUS: Snap! is on a public site and Sangala is a local // address, so the browser treats this as reaching into a private network - and it will not do // that for a synchronous request, which cannot be preflighted. So fire it off here and let the // next block wait for the answer. var stage = world.children[0].stage; if (!stage.trailsLog.length) { throw new Error('No pen vectors yet. Put "log pen vectors for Sangala" at the top of your script, then run your drawing.'); } var svg = stage.trailsLogAsPolySVG().src; window.sangala = {done: false, ok: false, why: ''}; // The helper takes the first free port from 8787 upward, so look for it rather than assume. function tryPort(p) { if (p > 8790) { window.sangala = {done: true, ok: false, why: window.sangala.why || 'no answer on ports 8787-8790'}; return; } fetch('http://127.0.0.1:' + p + '/snap/svg', {method: 'POST', body: svg}).then(function (r) { if (r.status === 200) { window.sangala = {done: true, ok: true, why: 'port ' + p}; } else { window.sangala.why = 'port ' + p + ' answered ' + r.status; tryPort(p + 1); } }).catch(function (e) { window.sangala.why = e.message; tryPort(p + 1); }); } tryPort(8787); return 'sending';

if (!window.sangala.ok) { throw new Error('Cannot reach Sangala Studio (' + window.sangala.why + '). Check that SangalaStudio.exe is running and rebuilt from the current source; that Snap! was opened from http://extensions.snap.berkeley.edu; and, if Chrome asks whether this site may reach devices on your local network, that you allowed it.'); } return 'sent to Sangala Studio on ' + window.sangala.why;
